title: A Thunderstorm In Town
content:
(A Reminiscence)



    She wore a new "terra-cotta" dress,
    And we stayed, because of the pelting storm,
    Within the hansom's dry recess,
    Though the horse had stopped; yea, motionless
        We sat on, snug and warm.

    Then the downpour ceased, to my sharp sad pain,
    And the glass that had screened our forms before
    Flew up, and out she sprang to her door:
    I should have kissed her if the rain
        Had lasted a minute more.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Hardy delivers a powerful performance in "A Thunderstorm In Town"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
